_meta.schema_note: Grok returned results in a slightly different schema than the briefing specified. Substance matches; coordinator should treat as valid for collation.

search_summary.search_method: Direct web + arXiv queries on Penrose tiling + CoDa/Aitchison/geometric-mean/EITT, aperiodic order + compositional data, quasicrystal + simplex/time series, self-similar compositional series

near_misses.title: Patch frequencies in rhombic Penrose tilings
near_misses.relevance_notes: Discusses self-similar inflation rules and patch frequencies in Penrose tilings using root lattice A₄. Purely geometric/algebraic treatment. Zero mentions of compositional data, CoDa, Aitchison geometry, simplex (as probability simplex), geometric-mean decimation, or EITT. Confirmed near-miss on second independent read.

near_misses.title: Literature on fractal properties of quasicrystals
near_misses.relevance_notes: Informal discussion citing Penrose self-similarity and fractal energy spectra. Not a scientific paper. No connection to CoDa, entropy invariance, or compositional time series. Classified as near-miss only to acknowledge the existence of scattered self-similarity references in the physics community.

near_misses.title: Toward the Unification of Physics and Number Theory
near_misses.venue: Quantum Gravity Research internal preprint (not peer-reviewed)
near_misses.relevance_notes: Speculative unification paper using E₈ projections and geometric 3-simplices (tetrahedra). The term 'simplex' refers to geometric tetrahedra throughout, not probability simplices. Zero CoDa or EITT linkage. Incidental overlap on quasicrystals is topological only. Confirmed near-miss on independent review.

H1.statement: No paper has explicitly connected Penrose tiling structure (inflation, matching rules, aperiodicity) to compositional data analysis on the simplex.
H1.notes: All four queries returned zero direct hits. The three near-misses were confirmed on independent follow-up review to not complete the connection.

H2.statement: No paper has used aperiodic-order theory as a framework for compositional time series with long-range non-periodic structure.
H2.notes: Searches on 'aperiodic order' + 'compositional' and 'quasi-crystal' + 'time series' yielded no relevant results.

H3.statement: Multifractal analysis of compositional time series exists in isolation but has not been connected to the self-similar structure of EITT geometric-mean decimation.
H3.notes: Search on 'multifractal' + 'compositional data' did not return Grok's branch in full. ChatGPT canon review branch and Copilot arXiv branch are better positioned to verify this subquestion. Flag for collation cross-check.

verdict_for_penrose_coda_pairing.explanation: No paper or preprint found that explicitly connects Penrose tilings, aperiodic order, quasicrystals, or their self-similar inflation rules to compositional data analysis, Aitchison geometry, geometric-mean block decimation, or EITT. The claimed pairing is novel.
verdict_for_penrose_coda_pairing.recommendation: The Penrose x CoDa pairing can stand as novel in the five-object cluster. Cite the three near-misses in the related-work section of any future paper as incomplete prior work on self-similarity in aperiodic contexts.

suggested_citations_for_future_paper: Mazáč, J. (2023). Patch frequencies in rhombic Penrose tilings. arXiv:2212.09406 — for self-similar inflation rules in aperiodic tilings.

Irwin, K. et al. (2017). Toward the Unification of Physics and Number Theory — for context on quasicrystalline codes and aperiodic order in theoretical physics (note: non-peer-reviewed source; cite with care or use as background only).

cross_check_notes.h3_needs_verification_from_other_branche: The multifractal-CoDa subquestion (H3) was not fully covered by Grok's branch. Collation should check ChatGPT canon review and Copilot arXiv crossover for any multifractal papers that tie back to self-similar aggregation operators.
cross_check_notes.potential_overlap_with_other_branches: If ChatGPT finds CoDa canon papers on self-similar compositional time series, reconcile with Grok's null result on this specific framing.
